assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md states '## Classification: code change request' and '## Action\nSub-task created: This code change request triggers creation of a Jira sub-task to wrap the three UPDATE statements in a database transaction (see subtask-30001.md).' The file subtask-30001.md exists with the corresponding sub-task content.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as a code change request (or upgraded suggestion) and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states '## Classification: code change request' and explains it was upgraded from suggestion: 'The initial classification could be considered a **suggestion** since the reviewer is proposing a performance optimization rather than identifying a functional bug. However, upon convention upgrade evaluation, this comment is upgraded to **code change request**.' The action section confirms: 'Sub-task created: This code change request triggers creation of a Jira sub-task to add a partial index on `deleted_at` in the migration (see subtask-30002.md).' The file subtask-30002.md exists.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md states '## Classification: nit' and '## Action\nNo sub-task created. Nit-level feedback does not trigger sub-task creation.' No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md states '## Classification: question' and '## Action\nNo sub-task created. Questions do not trigger sub-task creation -- they require a human response to clarify intent.' No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains sections: '## Repository' (trustify-backend), '## Description' (wrap three sequential UPDATE operations...), '## Files to Modify' (modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs), '## Implementation Notes' (Use SeaORM's transaction API...), '## Acceptance Criteria' (4 criteria listed). subtask-30002.md contains sections: '## Repository' (trustify-backend), '## Description' (Add a partial index on the deleted_at column...), '## Files to Modify' (migration/src/m0042_sbom_soft_delete/mod.rs), '## Implementation Notes' (Add the index after the existing alter_table call...), '## Acceptance Criteria' (4 criteria listed). Both files follow the template format.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR\nhttps://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'. subtask-30002.md contains '## Target PR\nhttps://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'. Both include the correct PR URL.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' with 'Comment ID: 30001', 'Author: reviewer-a', and the full original comment text starting with 'The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...' subtask-30002.md contains '## Review Context' with 'Comment ID: 30002', 'Author: reviewer-a', and the full original comment text starting with 'The migration should also add an index on `deleted_at` for the sbom table...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only analysis and reporting files: report.md, review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md, subtask-30001.md, subtask-30002.md. No code files (e.g., .rs, .py, .js files) exist in the outputs. The report describes reading and analyzing PR diffs but does not indicate any code modifications were made. Sub-task files describe what changes should be made but do not implement them.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have corresponding review classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report's Review Feedback Summary table lists all four comments with their classifications and sub-task decisions. The report's first row states '2 code change requests from reviewer-a; 2 sub-tasks created (transaction wrapping, migration index)', confirming all comments were processed.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report's overall status is 'WARN' with the note 'Two code change requests from reviewer-a require attention before merge. Sub-tasks have been created for both issues.' There is no mention of merging, auto-merging, or any merge action anywhere in the report. The report explicitly states issues require attention 'before merge', indicating the PR was not merged.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: The report contains a table row: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file with 5 new test functions; no existing tests modified or deleted |'. The detailed section further states: 'Classification: ADDITIVE (only new test functions added, no existing tests altered or removed)'.

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a detailed '### Convention Upgrade Evaluation' section with three evaluation criteria: (1) CONVENTIONS.md check noting 'the repository includes a CONVENTIONS.md file', (2) Codebase pattern check noting 'adding indexes in migrations alongside column additions is a well-established practice', and (3) Performance-related scrutiny noting the PR introduces the query pattern requiring the index. The report's Style/Conventions section also states: '1 suggestion was evaluated for convention upgrade; comment 30002 (adding index on deleted_at) was upgraded from suggestion to code change request' with supporting evidence about demonstrated project conventions.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path — whether classified directly as code change request based on reviewer language, or upgraded from suggestion via convention analysis
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md shows the comment was upgraded from suggestion to code change request via convention analysis: 'Upgrade decision: This suggestion is upgraded to code change request because adding indexes for frequently-filtered columns in migrations is a demonstrated project convention.' The action states: 'Sub-task created: This code change request triggers creation of a Jira sub-task to add a partial index on deleted_at in the migration (see subtask-30002.md).' The file subtask-30002.md exists with full sub-task content. The report's Review Feedback Summary confirms: '30002 | migration/src/m0042_sbom_soft_delete/mod.rs:14 | code change request (upgraded from suggestion) | Yes (subtask-30002)'.
